text.title: Methodologies Index Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Methodologies Index methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Every domain INDEX MUST partition methodologies into 3-8 groups; fewer leaves the list flat, more becomes a mini-tree.
rule.rationale: Partitioning is what turns L2 into a navigable mini decision-tree when N>50.

rule.statement: Every <methodology> row MUST carry complexity + produces attributes; rows without are rejected.
rule.rationale: Retriever uses these to balance bundle and skip wrong-shape outputs.

rule.statement: <summary> MUST state output produced (not topic), in <=200 chars.
Methodology rows MUST be alphabetically sorted by slug within each group.
count attr on <index> MUST equal the number of <methodology> entries.
rule.rationale: Mismatch signals an editor error; validator catches it.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
